answer:
Absolutely, I can think of a great example from a project I was involved with during college. So, it was our final year, and we had this massive group assignment for our marketing class. There were, let’s see, about five of us in the group, which was kind of a mixed bag. Everyone had different strengths, you know? 

We decided to meet up at a coffee shop to brainstorm ideas, and honestly, that’s when the magic happened. At first, we were all a bit disorganized—lots of talking over each other and tossing around ideas. But then, we settled down and started dividing tasks based on what everyone was good at. I focused on the presentation part, while others worked on the research and writing. 

As the deadline approached, we kept communicating through a group chat, just checking in and offering help where needed. It really felt like we were all in it together, pushing each other to do our best. In the end, we nailed it! The sense of accomplishment we felt was just incredible. Working together like that taught me a lot about collaboration and the power of teamwork.
title: Tell me about when you worked together with people to get something done.
